paper_metadata.title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
paper_metadata.abstract: We introduce LLaMA, a collection of foundation language models ranging from 7B to 65B parameters. We train our models on trillions of tokens, and show that it is possible to train state-of-the-art models using publicly available datasets exclusively, without resorting to proprietary and inaccessible datasets. In particular, LLaMA-13B outperforms GPT-3 (175B) on most benchmarks, and LLaMA-65B is competitive with the best models, Chinchilla-70B and PaLM-540B. We release all our models to the research community.

extraction_data.innovation: LLaMA introduces a collection of foundation language models ranging from 7B to 65B parameters, trained on publicly available datasets exclusively. The models achieve state-of-the-art performance with improved efficiency at inference, leveraging a Transformer decoder architecture and byte-pair encoding (BPE) tokenizer with specific modifications for number splitting and UTF-8 fallback.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: CommonCrawl, C4, Github, Wikipedia, Books (Gutenberg and Books3), ArXiv, StackExchange
extraction_data.extension: Byte-pair encoding (BPE) tokenizer splits numbers into individual digits and falls back to bytes for unknown UTF-8 characters, improving tokenization robustness.
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient and open large language models for diverse language understanding and generation tasks
extraction_data.application: Closed-book question answering, reading comprehension, mathematical reasoning, code generation, multitask language understanding, instruction following
extraction_data.paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models

extraction_data.innovation: Instruction-finetuned variant of LLaMA 65B, demonstrating improved performance on MMLU and instruction-following tasks with minimal finetuning on instruction data.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale text corpus including Common Crawl, Wikipedia, Books, ArXiv, and GitHub
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving instruction-following capabilities of large language models
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Instruction finetuning following the protocol of Chung et al. (2022)
extraction_data.extension: Byte-pair encoding (BPE) tokenizer splits numbers into individual digits and falls back to bytes for unknown UTF-8 characters, improving tokenization robustness.
extraction_data.paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
